title: Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Long Island Rail Road Company and Certain of Its Employees

note: Individual retirement exemption; the referenced EO 10530/10682 are cited only to preserve the Commission's delegated authority, not amended. Flag 6 fires (PRESENT) because the exemption is 'indefinite' with no expiration, unlike the fixed-term individual exemptions in this batch.

note: Ongoing fee-administration relationship exists (fees charged to applicants), so the compliance-regime flags are applicable and scored ABSENT rather than NOT_APPLICABLE; architecture is clean across all eleven.

title: Amending the Selective Service Regulations
note: Partial amendment of the standing Selective Service Regulations; the many cited predecessor orders (EO 10001 available, others such as 10008/10202 not needed since amended sections are self-contained), and the architecture is clean and rule-based throughout.

note: Pure one-time individual reinstatement waiver; per the mechanical test the compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) and Flags 6/9 are NOT_APPLICABLE, and the decision-structure flags are cleanly ABSENT.

note: An IEEPA sanctions order invoking an active national emergency; broad delegated licensing/rulemaking discretion drives several PRESENT findings, none rising to CRITICAL since the prohibition core is clearly defined and achievable.

note: Narrow statutory-designation order; the four compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) plus Flag 6 are NOT_APPLICABLE because the order creates no ongoing conduct rule of its own, applying an existing statute to one agency. All applicable flags are cleanly ABSENT.

note: Statutory Taft-Hartley board-of-inquiry order; compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) are NOT_APPLICABLE because the board only investigates and reports, creating no ongoing conduct rule. All applicable flags clean, including an explicit termination trigger clearing Flag 6.

title: Designating the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
note: Pure personnel-designation order with a fixed four-year term; Flags 4,5,8,9,10,11 are NOT_APPLICABLE as no compliance regime, systemic effect, or coverage rule exists. The fixed term cleanly clears Flag 6 (ABSENT).

title: Providing for the Restoration of Law and Order in the Washington Metropolitan Area
note: Emergency domestic military-deployment order; Flag 1 rated CRITICAL because indefinite, standardless deployment discretion pooled in the Defense Secretary is a core concentration. Flags 8 and 11 NOT_APPLICABLE as no ongoing compliance regime or coverage population exists.

title: Regulations Relating to the Right of Members of the Uniformed Services to Incentive Pay for the Performance of Hazardous Duty Required by Competent Orders
note: A long but architecturally clean implementing regulation for statutory hazardous-duty incentive pay; length reflects specificity, and every flag is answerable and ABSENT — precise criteria and distributed administration keep it at zero.

note: Program-termination/revocation order (revokes EO 12092); compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) and Flag 6 are NOT_APPLICABLE because the order dismantles rather than creates an ongoing rule or authority grant. All applicable flags cleanly ABSENT.

title: Delegating Authority of the President With Respect to Regulations Relating to Certain Allowances and Benefits to Government Personnel on Overseas Duty
note: Administrative delegation/consolidation order amending numerous predecessor orders (EO 10530, 10000, 10624, 10853, etc.); the only flag firing is Flag 1 for the aggregate self-executing delegation to the Secretary of State. note: Technical amendment package to the Manual for Courts-Martial amending a chain of predecessor orders (EO 11476 et seq.); the sole firing is Flag 7, for provisions expressly limiting judicial/appellate review of counsel-availability denials.

note: Technical amendment package to the 1984 Manual for Courts-Martial amending a chain of predecessor orders (EO 12473 et seq.); unlike order-031, these amendments contain no oversight-narrowing clause, so every applicable flag is cleanly ABSENT.

note: Wartime special-emergency-board order; Flags 1 and 4 fire on the Economic Stabilization Director's standardless override of the board's recommendations (Para 3). Flags 8 and 11 NOT_APPLICABLE (one-off determination, no ongoing compliance regime or coverage population).

title: Transfer of the Functions of the Smaller War Plants Corporation to the Reconstruction Finance Corporation and the Department of Commerce
note: Wartime agency-function-transfer reorganization; compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) and Flag 6 are NOT_APPLICABLE because the order is a one-time transfer creating no ongoing conduct rule or authority grant. note: Tax-return inspection-access order; Flags 7, 8, and 9 fire around the reduction of taxpayer-return confidentiality and the unverifiable-by-outsiders 'statistical purposes only' limit, none rising to CRITICAL given the bounded, statistically-limited scope.

note: Partial amendment adding two DoD units to EO 12171's labor-relations exclusion list (predecessor text available); Flag 7 fires for removing FLRA/Chapter 71 coverage and Flag 11 because the order is itself an exemption grant. Compliance-regime flags 4,5,8 NOT_APPLICABLE (a coverage exclusion, not an ongoing conduct rule).

note: Wartime maritime-function reorganization with an explicit statute-tied sunset (Sec. 9), clearing Flag 6; Flag 10 fires because it redirects operational functions away from the Bureau of Marine Inspection, Maritime Commission, and Collectors of Customs. Compliance-regime flags 4,5,8,11 NOT_APPLICABLE (a function transfer, not an ongoing conduct rule).

note: Routine FACA committee-continuance order superseding EO 12534 (predecessor text available and confirms the same continuance-by-supersession pattern); clean across all applicable flags with explicit termination dates and preserved congressional reporting.

note: Self-executing one-time land reservation under the Migratory Bird Conservation Act; compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11), sunset (6), effects (9), and cannibalization (10) genuinely inapplicable.

note: Diff-aware: predecessor EO-9001 was available. The amendment's key mechanism is a reporting requirement, and the diff weakens it—deleting mandatory quarterly reporting to the President in favor of self-maintained data and discretionary disclosure—driving the Flag 7 CRITICAL and the accountability/exemption findings.

note: Diff-aware: predecessor EO-10841 was available. note: Predecessors EO 8458, 8532, and 8743 were available and reviewed; diff-aware reading confirms this is a clean rule-bound wind-down of the Emergency Replacement List plus a fixed-class status conferral, with an ongoing but precisely-bounded eligibility determination that scores every applicable flag ABSENT.

note: Diff-aware partial amendment to EO 10001 §631.7 (predecessor text available); it closes an evasion loophole rather than weakening the mechanism. Cited older SSS EOs (10202, 10659, etc.) not in referenced-predecessors, but the operative baseline (10001) was available.

note: One-time statutory waiver under the Trade Act of 1974 (statute, not a numbered EO predecessor). Compliance-regime and lateral flags (4,5,8,10,11) NOT_APPLICABLE because no ongoing regulatory relationship or second agency exists; decision-structure flags score ABSENT, yielding a clean 0.00.

note: No numbered EO predecessor amended; scored on own text against the Defense Production Act of 1950. note: Wartime coordination office tied explicitly to the First War Powers Act's duration; mild power-concentration and vague-authority findings offset by the clear termination trigger and single named official.

note: Minimal single-function delegation to the Attorney General; per the compliance-regime rule, Flags 4, 5, 8, 11 are NOT_APPLICABLE (no ongoing regulatory relationship), and the remaining decision-structure/procedural flags all clear ABSENT. note: Wartime refinery seizure paralleling order-273; broad 'deems necessary' discretion plus Sec. 6 delegation to a Deputy drive Flags 1/2/4, while the explicit 60-day termination trigger clears Flag 6 and the active (unexpired) wartime statute means no zombie trap.

Interior's dual role drives Flag 1 and the multi-agency Scientific Authority plus coordination mandate drives Flag 10; undefined permit standards fire Flag 4.

title: Establishing Black Coulee Migratory Waterfowl Refuge, Montana
note: One-time self-executing land reservation; compliance-regime flags (4,5,8,11) and Flag 9 are genuinely NOT_APPLICABLE per the concrete test, while decision-structure flags score ABSENT.

title: Establishing the Division of Defense Aid Reports in the Office for Emergency Management of the Executive Office of the President
note: Internal EOP coordination body for Lend-Lease aid; compliance-regime flags NOT_APPLICABLE, but power concentration, lateral coordination friction, wartime-authority permanence, and downstream precedent all fire.

title: Conforming the Central Intelligence Agency and Civil Service Retirement and Disability Systems With Respect to Cost of Living Adjustments
note: Ongoing benefit-adjustment relationship exists (compliance-regime flags apply and score ABSENT), but the formula is fully mechanical and index-driven, so every flag clears cleanly.

title: Removing Wage and Salary Controls Adopted Pursuant to the Stabilization Act of 1942
note: Deregulatory one-time termination of wage controls; compliance-regime flags and forward-looking flags NOT_APPLICABLE because it dismantles rather than creates an ongoing regime, decision-structure flags ABSENT.

title: Federal Compliance With Pollution Control Standards
note: Revokes EO 11752 (predecessor text reviewed); the compliance-through-notification-and-negotiated-plan structure plus presidential exemption power and OMB conflict resolution drive multiple PRESENT flags, but none rises to core collapse (agencies remain substantively subject to the same standards as private persons).

title: Amending Executive Order No. 10450, as Amended, Relating to Security Requirements for Government Employment, and for Other Purposes
note: Diff-aware amendment of EO 10450 (predecessor reviewed); it deconcentrates and tightens the security-employment regime, so most flags clear, with residual bundling, still-imperfect adjudicative criterion, and precedential effects firing PRESENT. Revoked EO 11605 and EO 9835 texts not separately reviewed but their removal is deregulatory.

title: Adopting and Ratifying the Capture of the German Steamship "Milwaukee"
note: One-time self-executing prize ratification; compliance-regime and forward-looking flags NOT_APPLICABLE, decision-structure flags ABSENT.

title: Executive Office of the President (Office of Administration)
note: Internal EOP reorganization implementing Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1977; compliance-regime flags NOT_APPLICABLE, with vertical consolidation, lateral resource transfer, and institutional-precedent effects firing PRESENT.

title: Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons
note: Self-executing IOIA designation of the OPCW; compliance-regime flags NOT_APPLICABLE, decision-structure flags ABSENT, with only the downstream immunities effect firing Flag 9.

title: Amending Regulations Relating to the Safeguarding of Vessels, Harbors, Ports, and Waterfront Facilities of the United States
note: Partial amendment of EO 10173 (predecessor reviewed); the diff-aware read shows the amendment preserves and extends broad discretionary Commandant authority over a coverage/screening regime, firing power concentration, vague standards, exemption discretion, and related flags.

title: Blocking Libyan Government Property in the United States or Held by U.S. Persons
note: Extends/adds to EO 12543 (available in referenced-predecessors), which declared an active, unexpired Libya national emergency; the diff here is an additional blocking remedy, not a lapse-based continuation, so the Zombie Trap does not fire.

title: Termination of the Office of Strategic Services and Disposition of Its Functions
note: A one-time executive reorganization: the four compliance-regime flags (4, 5, 8, 11) are genuinely NOT_APPLICABLE since no ongoing regulatory relationship is created. Sec. 5 amends unnamed 'prior orders in conflict,' with no specific numbered predecessor to compare against.

title: Administration of Scientific Research by Agencies of the Federal Government
note: A structurally clean coordination framework: authority stays distributed among agency heads, NSF's role is advisory, and accountability runs to the President. Flag 6 clears under the standing-procedural-framework carve-out for a permanent administrative function.

title: Establishing the Tybee Migratory Bird Refuge
note: A one-time, self-executing land reservation under the Migratory Bird Conservation Act; the compliance-regime flags (4, 5, 8, 11) plus Flags 6 and 9 are genuinely NOT_APPLICABLE, while decision-structure Flags 1, 2, 7, Flag 3, and the multi-agency Flag 10 score cleanly ABSENT.

title: Continuing the Regulation of Exports
note: Textbook Zombie Emergency Trap: Sec. 1 explicitly continues authority 'Notwithstanding the expiration of the Export Administration Act of 1969' by re-anchoring in the 1917 Trading with the Enemy Act. Predecessors EO 11533, 11683, 11322, 11419 were all available in referenced-predecessors and confirm this is a wholesale continuation of the lapsed EAA administration framework, not a clean partial amendment.

title: Critical Infrastructure Protection
note: Structurally clean study/advisory order with explicit one-year and 180-day sunsets, FACA compliance, layered committee review, and an express non-supplant clause; only Flag 9 fires for the durable interagency infrastructure-protection precedent it establishes.

title: Withdrawal of Public Land for Use of the War Department
note: A one-time, self-executing temporary land withdrawal under the 1910/1912 withdrawal acts; compliance-regime flags (4, 5, 8, 11) plus Flags 6, 9, and 10 are genuinely NOT_APPLICABLE, while decision-structure Flags 1, 2, 7 and Flag 3 score cleanly ABSENT.
